title: T-062 — Reconcile scheduler→solver dispatch onto the canonical outbox/worker primitive
prompt:
# T-062 — Reconcile solver dispatch to outbox/worker

## Goal
The scheduler async path uses Postgres NOTIFY (T-012) + a LISTEN listener.py (T-021), which does not survive Vercel serverless boundaries and has no DLQ/retry/durability. 00-FOUNDATION already shipped the canonical durable async path: outbox + apps/worker + queue (T-111/T-112 with DLQ at attempts≥5, idempotency, retry). Closes 07-F3.

## Scope (pick one, document as an ADR note)
- Option A (preferred): dispatch via outbox event scheduler.run.requested → apps/worker consumer calls the deployed solver over authenticated HTTP (T-061). Update T-012 to enqueue an outbox event instead of NOTIFY; retire/repurpose listener.py (T-021) to a plain HTTP /solve handler.
- Option B (only if A rejected): keep LISTEN/NOTIFY but add a documented exception + recovery sweep + confirmation the listener runs in a persistent process (not Vercel).

## Acceptance criteria
- AC1 — Decision recorded (ADR-style) referencing T-111/T-112.
- AC2 — Option A: a run requested while the solver is down is retried and eventually delivered (or DLQ'd after N attempts) — integration test.
- AC3 — No reliance on a persistent LISTEN connection inside Vercel web functions; run-level idempotency (T-024) preserved end-to-end.

pipeline_inputs.description: Reconcile scheduler→solver dispatch from Postgres NOTIFY/LISTEN onto the canonical outbox + apps/worker primitive (T-111/T-112) for DLQ/retry/idempotency; or document an explicit exception. Source: sidecar m07-solver-dispatch-reconcile stub.

risk_red_lines: Do NOT introduce a third async mechanism.
Preserve at-least-once + idempotent-replay semantics of the outbox.
